senses.definition: the act or process of preparing a flower bud to open or the arrangement of petals before flowering
examples: The gardener studied the prefloration of the plant to ensure a smooth bloom.
In botany, prefloration refers to stages preceding true flowering.

senses.definition: plural of prefloration; multiple instances of preparing a flower bud to open or stages before flowering
examples: The study compared several preflorations across species.
Observations noted differences in preflorations among cultivars.

senses.definition: occurring before the plant starts to flower
examples: Preflowering shoots were observed to be shorter.
The dataset tracks preflowering stages in fruit trees.

senses.definition: to focus attention or preparation in advance of a task or event
examples: She prefocused on the key skills before starting the project.
We prefocus our efforts to improve early-stage outcomes.

senses.definition: having had focus arranged in advance; pre-oriented
examples: The prefocused strategy guided initial decisions.
A prefocused approach helps teams hit milestones early.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of prefocus; focuses in advance
examples: The team prefocuses its efforts on quality control.
She prefocuses resources before the launch.

senses.definition: the act of focusing in advance; preemptive focus
examples: Prefocusing is used to align team goals before decisions.
The study discusses prefocusing as a project planning step.

senses.definition: having been focused in advance; British spelling of prefocused
examples: The prefocussed approach streamlined early development.
A prefocussed plan reduces risk at launch.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of prefocuss; not common in modern usage but could mean to focus in advance
examples: The team prefocusses on early-stage risk assessment.

senses.definition: the act of focusing in advance; British spelling of prefocusing
examples: Prefocussing allowed the team to anticipate issues early.

senses.definition: the process of forming or adding leaves or leaf-like structures before full development
examples: The plant shows early prefoliation before leaf expansion.
Researchers study prefoliation patterns in vines.

senses.definition: plural of prefoliation; multiple instances of leaf formation events before full leaf development
examples: Different species show varying prefoliations.
The chart lists several prefoliations observed in the experiment.

senses.definition: to fool or deceive beforehand; not a common standard term, mainly historical or jocular
examples: The critic warned against prefooling the audience with false promises.

senses.definition: in a state before being forbidden; not standard; could be used in stylized writing
examples: The prefobidden policies were never enacted.

senses.definition: a context where forceps are prepared or used in advance; medical context
examples: The obstetric team prepared prefocrseps for potential delivery complications.
Preforceps preparation is standard in many procedures.

senses.definition: past tense of preforgive; to forgive in advance
examples: She preforgave him before he apologized.
They preforgave the minor mistake to keep the project moving.

senses.definition: to forgive in advance; to grant forgiveness before wrongdoing
examples: I preforgive you for any mistake you might make.
A preforgive policy can ease tense negotiations.

senses.definition: forgiven in advance; already forgiven beforehand
examples: The contract included preforgiven terms to reduce disputes.
She felt preforgiven for her upcoming decision.

senses.definition: the act of forgiving in advance; anticipatory forgiveness
examples: There is a culture of preforgiveness in some teams to maintain harmony.
Preforgiveness can prevent escalation of conflicts.

senses.definition: inclined to forgive in advance; forgiving beforehand
examples: Her prefogiving nature helped keep conversations calm.

senses.definition: before being forgotten; in a state prior to forgetfulness
examples: In the diary, events remained preforgotten until revealed.

senses.definition: an early or preliminary form or model from which something is made
examples: The metal object was manufactured from a preform.
Witnesses described the clay preform before shaping.

senses.definition: to shape or form beforehand; to make a preliminary form
examples: We preform the components before final assembly.

senses.definition: capable of being formed beforehand; formative
examples: The material is preformant, allowing easy shaping.

senses.definition: to arrange or set in a predefined format before use
examples: The template allows you to preformat text automatically.
Preformatting helps ensure consistency across documents.

senses.definition: the theory or state of something being formed beforehand; in biology, a developmental theory
examples: Historically, preformation was a debated theory in biology.
Preformation suggested organisms existed in miniature within gametes.
